claims.source_quote: Agriculture is the lifeline of Pakistan's economy accounting for 19.5 percent of the gross domestic product, employing 42.3 percent of the labour force and providing raw material for several value-added sectors.
claims.claim: Agriculture accounts for 19.5% of GDP, employs 42.3% of the labour force, and provides raw material to several value-added sectors, making it central to Pakistan's national development, food security, and poverty reduction.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Agriculture sector GDP share and labour force share

claims.source_quote: The rapid growth of Pakistan's urban areas indicate that demand for high-value perishable products such as fruits, vegetables, dairy, and meat is rising.
claims.claim: Rapid urbanisation in Pakistan is generating rising demand for high-value perishable products including fruits, vegetables, dairy, and meat.
claims.cause: rapid growth of Pakistan's urban areas
claims.effect: rising demand for high-value perishable products

claims.source_quote: Government is focusing to increase the yield for rural growers through major infrastructure investments including reliable transport networks and other building blocks for modern supply chains.
claims.claim: The Government is directing major infrastructure investments, including reliable transport networks, to increase yields for rural growers and develop modern supply chains.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Rural agricultural yield and supply chain infrastructure

claims.source_quote: CPEC will go a long way in the enhancement of agribusiness benefits by tapping value-added product innovation and supply chain.
claims.claim: CPEC is expected to substantially enhance agribusiness benefits through value-added product innovation and supply chain development.

claims.source_quote: performance of the agriculture sector remained up to the mark and achieved growth of 3.46 percent against the target of 3.5 percent and last year's meager growth of 0.27 percent.
claims.claim: The agriculture sector achieved 3.46% growth in 2016-17, nearly meeting the 3.5% target and substantially outperforming the previous year's meagre 0.27% growth.

claims.source_quote: This was possible by better harvesting of major crops through greater availability of agriculture inputs like water, agriculture credit and intensive fertilizers offtake.
claims.claim: Improved agriculture sector performance in 2016-17 was enabled by greater availability of inputs — water, agriculture credit, and fertilizers — which led to better harvesting of major crops.
claims.cause: greater availability of agriculture inputs like water, agriculture credit and intensive fertilizers offtake

claims.source_quote: The growth in crops was registered at 3.02 percent against the negative growth of 4.97 percent during the same period last year.
claims.claim: The crops sub-sector grew 3.02% in 2016-17, reversing a negative growth of 4.97% recorded in 2015-16.

claims.source_quote:
The growth of sub sector of important crops, other crops and cotton ginning posted growths of 4.12 percent, 0.21 percent and 5.59 percent, respectively against last year growths of -5.47 percent, 0.59 percent

and -22.12 percent respectively.
claims.claim: In 2016-17, important crops grew 4.12% (vs -5.47%), other crops 0.21% (vs 0.59%), and cotton ginning 5.59% (vs -22.12%) compared to 2015-16, reflecting broad recovery across crop sub-sectors.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Important crops, other crops, and cotton ginning growth rates

claims.source_quote: Maize production increased 16.30 percent against 6.77 percent last year, Sugarcane 12.41 percent against 4.23 percent, Cotton 7.59 percent against -28.96 and Rice 0.71 percent against -2.88 percent last year.
claims.claim: Production of Maize (+16.30%), Sugarcane (+12.41%), Cotton (+7.59%), and Rice (+0.71%) all grew in 2016-17, each outperforming or reversing their 2015-16 outcomes.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Major crop production growth: Maize, Sugarcane, Cotton, Rice

claims.source_quote: Only wheat production growth remained slightly low at 0.46 percent compared to 2.18 percent last year.
claims.claim: Wheat production growth slowed to 0.46% in 2016-17, down from 2.18% in 2015-16, making it the only major crop to underperform relative to the prior year.
claims.constraint_or_risk: wheat production growth significantly below prior year

claims.source_quote: Other crops accounted for 11.03 percent in value addition of agriculture grew by 0.21 percent during 201617 against 0.59 percent during the same period last year due to decline in the production of vegetables and oilseeds by posting negative growth of 0.73 percent and 5.93 percent, respectively.
claims.claim: Other crops' value-addition growth (11.03% share in agriculture) slowed to 0.21% in 2016-17 from 0.59% in 2015-16, caused by negative growth in vegetables (-0.73%) and oilseeds (-5.93%).
claims.indicator_or_subject: Other crops value addition growth; vegetables and oilseeds production
claims.constraint_or_risk: decline in production of vegetables and oilseeds
claims.cause: decline in the production of vegetables and oilseeds
claims.effect: slower growth in other crops value addition (0.21% vs 0.59% prior year)

claims.source_quote: Livestock accounts 58.33 percent share in the agriculture. It witnessed a growth of 3.43 percent compared to 3.36 percent during corresponding period last year.
claims.claim: Livestock, the largest agriculture sub-sector at 58.33% of value addition, grew 3.43% in 2016-17, marginally faster than 3.36% in 2015-16.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Livestock sub-sector growth rate and share

claims.source_quote: The Fishing sector contribution stands at 2.12 percent in agriculture value addition and recorded a growth of 1.23 percent compared to 3.25 percent growth of same period last year.
claims.claim: The Fishing sector, contributing 2.12% to agriculture value addition, slowed to 1.23% growth in 2016-17 from 3.25% in 2015-16.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Fishing sector growth rate and value-addition share

claims.source_quote: Forestry sector having contribution of 2.33 percent in the agriculture value addition witnessed a significant growth of 14.49 percent during 2016-17 due to higher timber production reported by Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
claims.claim: The Forestry sector, contributing 2.33% to agriculture value addition, recorded significant growth of 14.49% in 2016-17, driven by higher timber production in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Forestry sector growth rate and value-addition share
claims.cause: higher timber production reported by Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
claims.effect: significant growth of 14.49 percent in the Forestry sector
